question: What are the Basel III capital requirements for banks?
answer: The Basel III capital requirements for banks include a common equity tier 1 (CET1) capital ratio of 4.5%, a total capital ratio of 10.5%, and a leverage ratio of 3% [Wikipedia: Basel III]. The Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) has also introduced a capital conservation buffer of 2.5% and a countercyclical capital buffer of up to 2.5% [Wikipedia: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision]. These requirements are designed to ensure that banks have sufficient capital to absorb losses and maintain stability in the financial system [arXiv: 1903.05344].
